instruction:
Let \( n \) be any positive integer, and alpha be a real number, then
$$
[ \alpha]+\left[ \alpha+\frac{1} {n} \right]+\cdots+\left[ \alpha+\frac{n-1} {n} \right]=?
$$

Options:
A. $$
\left[ n + \alpha \right]
$$
B. $$
( \alpha n )
$$
C. $$
[ n \alpha]
$$
D. $$
\left( n \alpha \right)
$$
E. $$
n \left( \alpha \right)
$$
F. $$
[ \alpha + n]
$$
G. $$
(n \alpha)
$$
H. $$
[ \alpha n]
$$
I. $$
n \left[ \alpha \right]
$$
